creative_brief: Hero image: close-up of a hand holding unwashed carrots with visible soil, beside a small chalkboard showing a handwritten farm name and NSW postcode. Muted earth tones, natural light, no supermarket styling. Below the fold, a simple illustrated supply chain diagram: three steps (farm → hub → you) contrasted against a tangled six-step supermarket chain. Clean, editorial layout with generous whitespace.
headline: If you can't name the farm, you can't call it fresh
subhead: Every item in a supermarket trolley carries an invisible history — weeks in transit, multiple warehouses, anonymous hands. FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store where every product traces back to a named NSW farm, and you can verify it before you buy.
hero_copy: If your strawberries travel 2,400km through cold storage and three middlemen before reaching the shelf — then "fresh" is just a sticker, not a fact. If your eggs sit in a distribution centre for days before a truck moves them again — then the date on the carton is where transparency ends. FarmThru connects you directly to around 15–20 NSW partner farms and suppliers. No unnecessary middlemen. No anonymous supply chains. Order online, collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Every product, traceable. Every farm, named.

sections.heading: The maths behind your blind spot
sections.body:
If a head of lettuce passes through four hands before it reaches yours — grower, packer, distributor, retailer — then four times, someone decided it was "good enough" without asking you. You never met any of them. You never saw the soil. You washed it anyway, just in case.

That quiet ritual — rinsing everything, peeling what you shouldn't need to peel, cutting away bruised edges — is your body's way of admitting the system hasn't earned your trust. You're not paranoid. You're paying attention.

FarmThru's hub-and-collect model strips the chain back to its simplest form: farm to hub to you. Fewer hands. Fewer days. Fewer reasons to wonder.

sections.heading: What traceable actually looks like
sections.body:
We don't use "traceable" as a marketing word. We use it as an operating principle.

Our partner farms across NSW are listed — not hidden behind a wholesaler's invoice. When you order through FarmThru, you see where your food grew. Not a country of origin. A farm.

No subscriptions. No lock-in contracts. No membership fees. You order when you want, buy in bulk to save, and collect from our Brookvale hub at Unit 23, 10–18 Orchard Rd, Monday to Friday. We also offer delivery across Sydney, the Central Coast, and Wollongong.

If you wouldn't accept "somewhere in Australia" as an answer about your drinking water, why accept it for the food you feed your family every night?

sections.heading: Own a piece of the food system you actually want
sections.body:
FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal — and you can be part of it. Not just as a customer. As a partner.

A $5 refundable deposit gets you VIP access: first look at the campaign, priority updates, and an exclusive Q&A before anyone else. Minimum investment starts at $50, with a cap of $10,000 per investor.

This isn't about watching from the sidelines. It's about voting for change with your wallet — and owning a piece of what comes next.

Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. This is not a guaranteed return. Read the offer document carefully before committing any funds.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign will be conducted via Birchal. Any investment involves significant risks, including the potential loss of the entire amount invested. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. This page does not constitute financial advice. Prospective investors should read the offer document in full and consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision. The $5 VIP deposit is fully refundable.
